notes: MB returned only a search candidate (no release resolved): 'Indo-Jazz Suite' by 'Joe Harriott and John Mayer Double Quintet', firstReleaseDate 1966 — weak evidence, but it does corroborate the title, the 1966 year, John Mayer's involvement, and the 'double quintet' concept. No wikipedia evidence at all. Unchecked substantive claims: label Columbia; Mayer being an Indian violinist; 'first serious attempt to fuse jazz improvisation with Indian classical music'; 'Predated the Indo-jazz experiments of John McLaughlin and others by several years'; the five-jazz-plus-five-Indian-classical personnel split. No contradictions found.

Prose is verbatim from the stored wikipedia page (our album): fifth studio album, recorded and released 1966, full personnel (Morgan/Fuller/Hutcherson/Walton/Carter/Chambers), last Blue Note leader date until The State of the Tenor, Vols. 1 & 2. Embedded-MBID release (authoritative) confirms 1966, MPS 20628, artist credit, and all five keyTracks. The prose's bio sentence (full name 'William Henry Joseph Bonaparte Bertholf Smith', nickname 'the Lion', 'American jazz and stride pianist') is verbatim-verified against that artist page. The label field ships 'Unknown' while the embedded-MBID release (authoritative) says Atlantic 1460 — and our own prose already says 'released on the Atlantic label'. Fix the field to Atlantic. Year 1966 and all six keyTracks verified against MB. 'From the free jazz era... within the bebop conversation of the time' is internally muddled era boilerplate — rewrite candidate, not an evidence contradiction.

MB search matched the WRONG release: 'Sound Songs' (1997, Delmark), a different Roscoe Mitchell album — discarded for fact-checking, so there is no MB evidence for 'Sound'. Verification rests on the stored wikipedia page, which IS our album: 1966 debut on Delmark; personnel includes Lester Bowie and Malachi Favors (six musicians total, consistent with 'Sextet'); the original-LP track listing covers all three keyTracks (Ornette / The Little Suite / Sound), so keyTracks are marked ok on wiki evidence. Unchecked substantive claims: 'The first recording by the AACM' and 'founding document of the AACM' — the wiki extract never mentions the AACM; the claim is widely repeated per background knowledge, but that is not citable here. 'Spawned the Art Ensemble of Chicago' is looser than the wiki passage (AEC recordings 'expanded upon' Sound's concepts). The silence/space characterization is supported by the Penguin/AllMusic quotes in the wiki extract.

Prose is verbatim from the stored wikipedia page (our album): sixth album, released June 1966 by Blue Note, hard bop + modal, personnel Hubbard/Hancock/Carter/Elvin Jones, cover photo of first wife Teruko (Irene) Nakagami met 1961. Ours consistently says 1966 and sides with the more detailed wiki account — flagged for human review, not charged as an error against ours.

notes: The internal inconsistency is the certain defect: year field 1967 vs prose 'released... in January 1968'. The embedded MB release (a DE pressing, BST 84271) and its release-group both say 1967, which the pack rules treat as authoritative — but a January 1968 release for an album recorded October 27, 1967 is plausible, and MB year-granularity dates are often imprecise, so repair should re-source rather than blindly trust 1967. All five keyTracks match the MB tracklist exactly. Unchecked claims (no personnel in evidence): recording date October 27, 1967; Blue Mitchell, Lonnie Smith, Jimmy Ponder, Idris Muhammad.

Pack evidence (embedded MBID) supports 1967 via the release-group first release date, but the matched release itself is a 2004 digital reissue and MB first-release dates can lag reality; the prose reads like it was lifted from a wikipedia article, so repair should re-source the original Blue Note release year externally. All three keyTracks match the MB tracklist ('Coppin' the Haven' matches up to apostrophe style). Unchecked claims (no personnel or session data in evidence): June 2, 1964 Paris recording; Donald Byrd, Kenny Drew, Niels-Henning Orsted Pedersen, Art Taylor.

MB search matched the WRONG entity: 'Straight No Chaser: Music from the Motion Picture' (1989 documentary soundtrack) — discarded; no usable MB evidence. Verification rests on the stored wikipedia page, which IS our album: sixth Columbia studio album, released 1967, 1996 CD reissue with restored versions and additional tracks (our first two prose sentences are verbatim from it). KeyTracks verified against the wiki track listing: 'Straight, No Chaser', 'Japanese Folk Song', and 'We See' on the original LP; 'Green Chimneys' appears only on the 1996 CD reissue listing — acceptable as a key track but worth knowing. 'Mature quartet in peak form' is editorial and consistent with the wiki personnel (Monk/Rouse/Gales/Riley, a quartet).
